info.title: PalLLM sidecar API
info.description: Local-first companion-runtime HTTP surface. Application routes live under /api; operational routes (/metrics, /health/*, /openapi/*) and the MCP transport endpoint (/mcp) are intentionally separate from the JSON API namespace. Bridge-specific world snapshot internals are published under neutral schema ids so external clients do not have to couple to the current game target.

get.summary: Get the current runtime health snapshot.
Get the aggregated dashboard snapshot used by the field console.
List the shipped feature catalog entries.
One-shot self-description manifest for AI / MCP consumers.
Live state-aware next-step guidance for humans + AI.
Latest SelfHealingWorker evidence or a pending marker when the watchdog has not ticked yet.
Classify every outbound surface so operators + AI can prove air-gap posture.
Coverage of the Edge / Worker / Judge / Media / Validator roles in the local-first AI mesh.
Detected hardware posture: CPU cores, RAM, GPU-likelihood, and recommended DuoHardwareTier. Honours PalLLM:Hardware:ForceTier override.
Advisory posture for the current hardware + options: CPU-only deterministic-first, entry-GPU worker-only, full-mesh no-degradation. Never mutates runtime state.
Resource-budget posture per feature (inference rate, vision queue, TTS caps, memory window, bridge retention, fallback share) with ok / review / exhausted bucketing.
Should the companion narrate right now? Deterministic decision from the current world snapshot. Never calls inference.
Cross-session lifetime summary for every tracked character. Reads the persisted aggregate under Runtime/LifetimeRelationships/latest.json with bounded local JSON ingress.
Deterministic mood-weather forecast for a tracked character, blended from relationship + world snapshot.
Enumerate every data-emitting surface and classify it as never-leaves / only-with-opt-in / leaves-by-default. Pairs with /api/airgap/verify.

post.summary: Pass 34 – fan a single utterance out across multiple character ids. Each per-character reply runs through the full ChatAsync pipeline.
Translate a player utterance into an ordered plan of allowlisted pal directives. Deterministic — no inference call. Never emits above AutomationOptions.AllowedActions.
Recommend a Qwen Duo cooperation pattern for the given task / risk / hardware.
Compare two model outputs and emit a structured disagreement verdict + safety signal.
Build a machine-readable proof packet for an automated decision (provenance bundle with rollback).
Record one observation against the hard-code promotion ledger.

get.summary: Per-task-class promotion summary; flags patterns stable enough to hard-code.
Hard-code suggestions for every promotion candidate: target file, suggested change, evidence summary, rollback path, ProofPacket.

post.summary: Build an editor-ready change template for a specific promotion candidate.
Persist a promotion candidate as a staging-only template + rollback + provenance triple under the configured staging root. Never mutates source code. Gated by PalLLM:PromotionApply:AllowApply.

get.summary: Get a machine-readable release/readiness snapshot with canonical audit commands, doc pointers, and publication blockers.
Get a machine-readable Palworld bridge proof snapshot with native readiness, widget-seam evidence, and live request/delivery closure.
Get a recent per-model live inference summary with latency-budget assessment and token trends for the active GenAI lanes.
Get a hardware-aware collaboration plan for the configured local model lanes.

post.summary: Plan the exact dense-plus-MoE operating strategy for a concrete task and hardware profile.
Prime the currently active inference model with a bounded low-token warmup request.

get.summary: List the discovered snapshots of configured upstream MCP servers.
List the narrative packs currently loaded by the runtime.
Pick the personality-pack id that should apply to a character of the given species (operator-configured species map, then optional caller fallback).
Get the recent adapter log tail.
Get the current world snapshot plus bridge activity.

post.summary: Reload narrative packs from disk.
Validate a narrative pack payload without loading it.
Push a new game snapshot into the runtime.
Drain the bridge inbox immediately.

get.summary: List the pending outbox envelopes waiting for a game-side consumer.
Get ranked UI widget probe diagnostics from bridge dumps.

post.summary: Clear every pending outbox envelope.
Run a single chat turn through the PalLLM orchestration pipeline.
Server-Sent-Events variant of /api/chat. Emits 'started' / 'phase' / 'final' (or 'error') events so clients see progress before the final answer lands.
Return the Duo cooperation pattern the planner would pick for a specific chat request, plus the executable role chain that would dispatch today.
Answer a natural-language causal question about the runtime's recent behaviour.
Recall scored memory matches for a query.

get.summary: List every tracked per-character relationship.
Get the tracked relationship for a single character id.

post.summary: Generate a freeform scene description for an image.
Extract structured world-state data from an image and optionally merge it into the live snapshot.
Process pending screenshots from the bridge screenshot inbox.
Persist the current session state to disk.
Reload the session state from disk.
Synthesize speech audio for a text payload.
Transcribe a bounded local audio payload through an opt-in OpenAI-compatible ASR endpoint.

ChatLatencyHistogram.description:
Cumulative Prometheus-style histogram record. double ChatLatencyHistogram.SumSeconds
            + long ChatLatencyHistogram.Count give average; each LatencyHistogramBucket
            entry records "count of observations with duration <= UpperBoundSeconds".

InferredTaskKind.description:
Advisory — the DuoTaskKind the Pass-16 ChatTaskKindInferer
assigned to this turn. Null on turns where the runtime skipped
advisory inference (rate-limited, missing DI, etc.). Purely
observational: chat routing has already happened by the time
this lands in the response.

CooperationPattern.description:
Advisory — the DuoCooperationPattern the Pass-8 planner picked
for this turn given the inferred task kind + current role
coverage. Populated on every chat turn starting Pass 21; null
only when the planner throws (which is deterministic so should
never happen in practice). Does NOT affect actual inference
routing today — the single-lane `_inferenceClient` still
handles every dispatch. Pass 22 added `DispatchedRoleChain` to
expose the concrete role chain the planner's pattern would
invoke once multi-role dispatch lands.

DispatchedRoleChain.description:
Advisory — the ordered role chain the Pass-22 ChatDispatchPlanner
would invoke for this turn, given current role bindings + the
planner's pattern. Empty when the planner chose
DeterministicOnly or both roles are unbound. The runtime still
dispatches through the single-lane inference client today, so
this is observational; the field is here so operators + AI
agents can see the concrete execution plan and so a future pass
can flip the single-lane passthrough to actually invoke the
chain recorded here without a breaking contract change.

DispatchMode.description:
Advisory — short dispatch-mode bucket ("deterministic-only",
"single-role", "duo-sequential", "duo-parallel", "duo-fanout",
"duo-tournament", "duo-background", "duo-watchdog",
"duo-appeal"). Correlates with the Pass-22
`ChatDispatchDecision.Mode`. Null when no decision was
captured for this turn.

AddressedPal.description: Optional pal name the utterance addresses.

DuoTaskKind.description:
The task shape the caller wants to accomplish. Used by the planner
to pick the right cooperation pattern. Derived from the 2035
architecture brief's "best division of labor" table so every common
game / app / coding / media task has a named shape.

FallbackStrategyCount.description: Count of chat replies served by a single fallback strategy since startup.

GameBaseSnapshot.title: GameBaseSnapshot
GameBaseSnapshot.description:
Internal bridge-authored record for one known base / camp / outpost in the
current world snapshot. Published through the neutral
`GameBaseSnapshot` schema id on the public OpenAPI surface.

GameCharacterSnapshot.title: GameCharacterSnapshot
GameCharacterSnapshot.description:
Internal bridge-authored character snapshot.

The current live bridge is game-specific, but the publication-facing
HTTP/OpenAPI contract exposes this shape under the neutral
GameCharacterSnapshot schema id so external consumers can depend on
a target-agnostic contract.

GameWorldSnapshot.title: GameWorldSnapshot
GameWorldSnapshot.description:
Internal bridge-authored snapshot of the current game world.

The sidecar currently fills this from the shipped Palworld bridge,
but the publication-facing HTTP/OpenAPI contract exposes the same shape
under the neutral GameWorldSnapshot schema id.

HealthSuggestion.description:
One actionable next-step hint surfaced in IReadOnlyList<HealthSuggestion> RuntimeHealth.Suggestions.
            string HealthSuggestion.Code is a stable kebab-case identifier (e.g. "no-packs-loaded",
            "inference-circuit-open", "bridge-idle") so dashboards and CI tooling can
            match on it without parsing the Message.string HealthSuggestion.Message is a single sentence explaining the situation in plain
            English.string? HealthSuggestion.Command is an optional copy-paste command (a pal verb,
            a PowerShell one-liner, etc.) the operator can run to address it. Null when no
            single-shot remediation exists.string HealthSuggestion.Severity is one of "info" / "warn" / "urgent"
            and lets every consumer (dashboard, MCP client, pal next, pal doctor) render
            matching visual treatment without duplicating a code-to-severity map. The builder
            is the source of truth so a new hint code automatically lights up the right colour
            across every surface without coordinated edits.

ModelTierTransitionCount.description: Count of times the tier orchestrator graduated from one tier id to another.

CharacterIds.description: Character ids to fan the utterance out to, in order.

CharacterNames.description: Optional parallel list of names (same length as List<int> PartyChatRequest.CharacterIds). When omitted, names come from the adapter.

TaskTag.description: Task tag applied to every per-character request.

Threaded.description: When true, each reply is seeded with a brief mention of earlier characters' replies so a conversation thread forms. Default off — each character replies independently.

Temperature.description: Optional temperature passed to every turn. When omitted, per-character defaults apply.

PartyChatRequest.description:
Pass 34 / C1 — request shape for `POST /api/chat/party`.
Fans out a single utterance across several character ids so a
whole party of companions can reply to the same player prompt in
sequence. Uses the existing ChatRequest machinery
per-character — so every reply still goes through the task-aware
execution profile, the Pass-8 planner, rate limiting, and
deterministic fallback.

Deliberately NOT a contract change on ChatRequest:
the per-turn shape stays identical so single-character callers
see zero behaviour change. Party chat is an orchestration on top.

PromotionApplyPreviewRequest.description:
Wire-level request shape for `POST /api/promotion/apply/preview`.
Callers name the task class + pattern id they want a preview for;
the server looks up the matching candidate in the live ledger and
builds the preview from the derived PromotionSuggestion.

TaskClass.description: Task class to promote. Must match a live ledger candidate.

PatternId.description: Optional pattern id filter — when omitted, the highest-count pattern for the task is used.

FallbackStrategyCounts.description:
Per-strategy usage counter from the deterministic fallback director.
            Rendered as labeled Prometheus counters (`palllm_fallback_strategy_total{strategy="..."}`).
            Empty dictionary when no fallback replies have been served yet.

ModelTierTransitionCounts.description:
Per-transition counter for model-tier graduations. Each entry records
            a directional transition (for example, `small -> large`) along with how many times that
            transition fired since startup. Rendered as labeled Prometheus counters
            (`palllm_model_tier_transition_total{from="...",to="..."}`).

ChatLatency.description:
Cumulative Prometheus-style histogram of end-to-end chat latency
            in seconds. Buckets cover the realistic range for PalLLM: sub-10ms for
            fallback-only paths, up to 60s for inference-backed paths on large models.

Suggestions.description:
Operator-actionable next-step hints derived from the current snapshot.
            Empty when the runtime is healthy. Each entry carries a stable `Code` for
            programmatic consumption (dashboards, `pal next` advisor, MCP tools), a
            human-readable `Message`, and an optional `Command` the operator can
            copy-paste to address it. This is the single source of "what should I do
            right now?" for both /api/health curl callers and the Field Console.
